# frozen_string_literal: true

module RubyLLM
  module Providers
    class OpenRouter
      # Streaming methods of the OpenRouter API integration
      module Streaming
        module_function

        def build_chunk(data)
          usage = data['usage'] || {}
          delta = data.dig('choices', 0, 'delta') || {}

          Chunk.new(
            role: :assistant,
            model_id: data['model'],
            content: delta['content'],
            thinking: Thinking.build(
              text: extract_thinking_text(delta),
              signature: extract_thinking_signature(delta)
            ),
            tool_calls: parse_tool_calls(delta['tool_calls'], parse_arguments: false),
            input_tokens: input_tokens(usage),
            output_tokens: output_tokens(usage),
            cached_tokens: cache_read_tokens(usage),
            cache_creation_tokens: cache_write_tokens(usage),
            thinking_tokens: thinking_tokens(usage)
          )
        end
      end
    end
  end
end
=== gpustack/models ===
# frozen_string_literal: true

require 'time'

module RubyLLM
  module Providers
    class GPUStack
      # Models methods of the GPUStack API integration
      module Models
        module_function

        def models_url
          'models'
        end

        def parse_list_models_response(response, slug, _capabilities)
          items = response.body['items'] || []
          items.map do |model|
            Model::Info.new(
              id: model['name'],
              name: model['name'],
              created_at: model['created_at'] ? Time.parse(model['created_at']) : nil,
              provider: slug,
              family: 'gpustack',
              metadata: {
                description: model['description'],
                source: model['source'],
                huggingface_repo_id: model['huggingface_repo_id'],
                ollama_library_model_name: model['ollama_library_model_name'],
                backend: model['backend'],
                meta: model['meta'],
                categories: model['categories']
              },
              context_window: model.dig('meta', 'n_ctx'),
              max_output_tokens: model.dig('meta', 'n_ctx'),
              capabilities: build_capabilities(model),
              modalities: build_modalities(model),
              pricing: {}
            )
          end
        end

        private

        def build_capabilities(model) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
          capabilities = []

          # Add streaming by default for LLM models
          capabilities << 'streaming' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')

          # Map GPUStack metadata to standard capabilities
          capabilities << 'function_calling' if model.dig('meta', 'support_tool_calls')
          capabilities << 'vision' if model.dig('meta', 'support_vision')
          capabilities << 'reasoning' if model.dig('meta', 'support_reasoning')

          # GPUStack models generally support structured output and json mode
          capabilities << 'structured_output' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
          capabilities << 'json_mode' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')

          capabilities
        end

        def build_modalities(model)
          input_modalities = []
          output_modalities = []

          if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
            input_modalities << 'text'
            input_modalities << 'image' if model.dig('meta', 'support_vision')
            input_modalities << 'audio' if model.dig('meta', 'support_audio')
            output_modalities << 'text'
          elsif model['categories']&.include?('embedding')
            input_modalities << 'text'
            output_modalities << 'embeddings'
          end

          {
            input: input_modalities,
            output: output_modalities
          }
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

                     :deployment_base
                   elsif path_base.include?('/openai/v1')
                     :openai_v1_base
                   else
                     :resource_base
                   end

            {
              raw_base: raw_base,
              path_base: path_base,
              root: azure_host_root(path_base),
              mode: mode,
              version: version
            }
          end
        end

        def chat_endpoint(parts)
          case parts[:mode]
          when :chat_endpoint
            ''
          when :deployment_base
            with_api_version('chat/completions', parts[:version] || AZURE_DEFAULT_CHAT_API_VERSION)
          when :openai_v1_base
            with_api_version('chat/completions', parts[:version])
          else
            with_api_version('models/chat/completions', parts[:version] || AZURE_DEFAULT_CHAT_API_VERSION)
          end
        end

        def embeddings_endpoint(parts)
          case parts[:mode]
          when :deployment_base, :openai_v1_base
            with_api_version('embeddings', parts[:version])
          else
            "#{parts[:root]}/openai/v1/embeddings"
          end
        end

        def models_endpoint(parts)
          case parts[:mode]
          when :openai_v1_base
            with_api_version('models', parts[:version] || AZURE_DEFAULT_MODELS_API_VERSION)
          else
            "#{parts[:root]}/openai/v1/models?api-version=#{parts[:version] || AZURE_DEFAULT_MODELS_API_VERSION}"
          end
        end

        def with_api_version(path, version)
          return path unless version

          separator = path.include?('?') ? '&' : '?'
          "#{path}#{separator}api-version=#{version}"
        end

        def azure_host_root(base_without_query)
          base_without_query.sub(%r{/(models|openai)/.*\z}, '').sub(%r{/+\z}, '')
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

=== openrouter.rb ===
# frozen_string_literal: true

module RubyLLM
  module Providers
    # OpenRouter API integration.
    class OpenRouter < Provider
      # OpenRouter's dialect of the Chat Completions API.
      class ChatCompletions < Protocols::ChatCompletions
        include OpenRouter::Chat
        include OpenRouter::Images
        include OpenRouter::Models
        include OpenRouter::Streaming
      end

      protocol :chat_completions, ChatCompletions

      def api_base
        @config.openrouter_api_base || 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'
      end

      def headers
        {
          'Authorization' => "Bearer #{@config.openrouter_api_key}"
        }
      end

      def parse_error(response)
        return if response.body.empty?

        body = try_parse_json(response.body)
        case body
        when Hash
          parse_error_part_message body
        when Array
          body.map do |part|
            parse_error_part_message part
          end.join('. ')
        else
          body
        end
      end

      class << self
        def configuration_options
          %i[openrouter_api_key openrouter_api_base]
        end

        def configuration_requirements
          %i[openrouter_api_key]
        end
      end

      private

      def parse_error_part_message(part)
        message = part.dig('error', 'message')
        raw = try_parse_json(part.dig('error', 'metadata', 'raw'))
        return message unless raw.is_a?(Hash)

        raw_message = raw.dig('error', 'message')
        return [message, raw_message].compact.join(' - ') if raw_message

        message
      end
    end
  end
end
=== openrouter/chat ===
# frozen_string_literal: true

module RubyLLM
  module Providers
    class OpenRouter
      # Chat methods of the OpenRouter API integration
      module Chat
        module_function

        # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
        def render_payload(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, stream: false, schema: nil,
                           thinking: nil, citations: false, tool_prefs: nil)
          payload = super
          payload.delete(:reasoning_effort)
          strip_schema_strict(payload)

          reasoning = build_reasoning(thinking)
          payload[:reasoning] = reasoning if reasoning
          payload
        end
        # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists

        def strip_schema_strict(payload)
          schema_def = payload.dig(:response_format, :json_schema, :schema)
          return unless schema_def.is_a?(Hash)

          schema_def = RubyLLM::Utils.deep_dup(schema_def)
          schema_def.delete(:strict)
          schema_def.delete('strict')
          payload[:response_format][:json_schema][:schema] = schema_def
        end

        def build_reasoning(thinking)
          return nil unless thinking&.enabled?

          reasoning = {}
          reasoning[:effort] = thinking.effort if thinking.respond_to?(:effort) && thinking.effort
          reasoning[:max_tokens] = thinking.budget if thinking.respond_to?(:budget) && thinking.budget
          reasoning[:enabled] = true if reasoning.empty?
          reasoning
        end

        def format_thinking(msg)
          thinking = msg.thinking
          return {} unless thinking && msg.role == :assistant

          details = []
          if thinking.text
            details << {
              type: 'reasoning.text',
              text: thinking.text,
              signature: thinking.signature
            }.compact
          elsif thinking.signature
            details << {
              type: 'reasoning.encrypted',
              data: thinking.signature
            }
          end

          details.empty? ? {} : { reasoning_details: details }
        end

        def extract_thinking_text(message_data)
          candidate = message_data['reasoning']
          return candidate if candidate.is_a?(String)

          details = message_data['reasoning_details']
          return nil unless details.is_a?(Array)

          text = details.filter_map do |detail|
            case detail['type']
            when 'reasoning.text'
              detail['text']
            when 'reasoning.summary'
              detail['summary']
            end
          end.join

          text.empty? ? nil : text
        end

        def extract_thinking_signature(message_data)
          details = message_data['reasoning_details']
          return nil unless details.is_a?(Array)

          signature = details.filter_map do |detail|
            detail['signature'] if detail['signature'].is_a?(String)
          end.first
          return signature if signature

          encrypted = details.find { |detail| detail['type'] == 'reasoning.encrypted' && detail['data'].is_a?(String) }
          encrypted&.dig('data')
        end
      end
    end
  end
end
=== openrouter/streaming ===
# frozen_string_literal: true

module RubyLLM
  module Providers
    class OpenRouter
      # Streaming methods of the OpenRouter API integration
      module Streaming
        module_function

        def build_chunk(data)
          usage = data['usage'] || {}
          delta = data.dig('choices', 0, 'delta') || {}

          Chunk.new(
            role: :assistant,
            model_id: data['model'],
            content: delta['content'],
            thinking: Thinking.build(
              text: extract_thinking_text(delta),
              signature: extract_thinking_signature(delta)
            ),
            tool_calls: parse_tool_calls(delta['tool_calls'], parse_arguments: false),
            input_tokens: input_tokens(usage),
            output_tokens: output_tokens(usage),
            cached_tokens: cache_read_tokens(usage),
            cache_creation_tokens: cache_write_tokens(usage),
            thinking_tokens: thinking_tokens(usage)
          )
        end
      end
    end
  end
end
=== gpustack/models ===
# frozen_string_literal: true

require 'time'

module RubyLLM
  module Providers
    class GPUStack
      # Models methods of the GPUStack API integration
      module Models
        module_function

        def models_url
          'models'
        end

        def parse_list_models_response(response, slug, _capabilities)
          items = response.body['items'] || []
          items.map do |model|
            Model::Info.new(
              id: model['name'],
              name: model['name'],
              created_at: model['created_at'] ? Time.parse(model['created_at']) : nil,
              provider: slug,
              family: 'gpustack',
              metadata: {
                description: model['description'],
                source: model['source'],
                huggingface_repo_id: model['huggingface_repo_id'],
                ollama_library_model_name: model['ollama_library_model_name'],
                backend: model['backend'],
                meta: model['meta'],
                categories: model['categories']
              },
              context_window: model.dig('meta', 'n_ctx'),
              max_output_tokens: model.dig('meta', 'n_ctx'),
              capabilities: build_capabilities(model),
              modalities: build_modalities(model),
              pricing: {}
            )
          end
        end

        private

        def build_capabilities(model) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
          capabilities = []

          # Add streaming by default for LLM models
          capabilities << 'streaming' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')

          # Map GPUStack metadata to standard capabilities
          capabilities << 'function_calling' if model.dig('meta', 'support_tool_calls')
          capabilities << 'vision' if model.dig('meta', 'support_vision')
          capabilities << 'reasoning' if model.dig('meta', 'support_reasoning')

          # GPUStack models generally support structured output and json mode
          capabilities << 'structured_output' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
          capabilities << 'json_mode' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')

          capabilities
        end

        def build_modalities(model)
          input_modalities = []
          output_modalities = []

          if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
            input_modalities << 'text'
            input_modalities << 'image' if model.dig('meta', 'support_vision')
            input_modalities << 'audio' if model.dig('meta', 'support_audio')
            output_modalities << 'text'
          elsif model['categories']&.include?('embedding')
            input_modalities << 'text'
            output_modalities << 'embeddings'
          end

          {
            input: input_modalities,
            output: output_modalities
          }
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

SKIP_LOCAL_PROVIDER_TESTS = ENV['SKIP_LOCAL_PROVIDER_TESTS'].to_s.match?(/\A(1|true|yes)\z/i)
LOCAL_PROVIDER_SLUGS = %i[ollama gpustack].freeze

def filter_local_providers(models)
  SKIP_LOCAL_PROVIDER_TESTS ? models.reject { |model| LOCAL_PROVIDER_SLUGS.include?(model[:provider]) } : models
end

chat_models = [
  { provider: :anthropic, model: 'claude-haiku-4-5' },
  { provider: :azure, model: 'grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning' },
  { provider: :bedrock, model: 'amazon.nova-2-lite-v1:0' },
  { provider: :deepseek, model: 'deepseek-chat' },
  { provider: :gemini, model: 'gemini-2.5-flash' },
  { provider: :gpustack, model: 'qwen3' },
  { provider: :mistral, model: 'mistral-small-latest' },
  { provider: :ollama, model: 'qwen3' },
  { provider: :openai, model: 'gpt-5-nano' },
  { provider: :openrouter, model: 'claude-haiku-4-5' },
  { provider: :perplexity, model: 'sonar' },
  { provider: :vertexai, model: 'gemini-2.5-flash' },

# frozen_string_literal: true

RubyLLM.configure do |config|
  config.model_registry_class = 'Model'
  config.max_retries = 0
  config.retry_backoff_factor = 0
  config.retry_interval = 0
  config.retry_interval_randomness = 0
end

RSpec.shared_context 'with configured RubyLLM' do
  before do
    RubyLLM.configure do |config|
      config.anthropic_api_key = ENV.fetch('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY', 'test')
      config.azure_api_base = 'https://rubyllm.services.ai.azure.com'
      config.azure_api_key = ENV.fetch('AZURE_API_KEY', 'test')
      config.bedrock_api_key = ENV.fetch('AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID', 'test')
      config.bedrock_region = 'us-west-2'
      config.bedrock_secret_key = ENV.fetch('AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY', 'test')
      config.bedrock_session_token = ENV.fetch('AWS_SESSION_TOKEN', nil)
      config.deepseek_api_base = ENV.fetch('DEEPSEEK_API_BASE', nil)
      config.deepseek_api_key = ENV.fetch('DEEPSEEK_API_KEY', 'test')
      config.gemini_api_key = ENV.fetch('GEMINI_API_KEY', 'test')
      config.gpustack_api_base = ENV.fetch('GPUSTACK_API_BASE', 'http://localhost:11444/v1')
      config.gpustack_api_key = ENV.fetch('GPUSTACK_API_KEY', nil)
      # Disable retries in tests for deterministic, fast failures.
      config.max_retries = 0
      config.mistral_api_key = ENV.fetch('MISTRAL_API_KEY', 'test')
      config.model_registry_class = 'Model'
      config.ollama_api_base = ENV.fetch('OLLAMA_API_BASE', 'http://localhost:11434/v1')
      config.ollama_api_key = ENV.fetch('OLLAMA_API_KEY', nil)
      config.openai_api_key = ENV.fetch('OPENAI_API_KEY', 'test')
      config.openrouter_api_key = ENV.fetch('OPENROUTER_API_KEY', 'test')
      config.perplexity_api_key = ENV.fetch('PERPLEXITY_API_KEY', 'test')
      config.request_timeout = 600
      config.retry_backoff_factor = 0
      config.retry_interval = 0
      config.retry_interval_randomness = 0
      config.vertexai_location = ENV.fetch('GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION', 'global')
      config.vertexai_project_id = ENV.fetch('GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT', 'test-project')
      config.vertexai_service_account_key = ENV.fetch('VERTEXAI_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY', nil)
      config.xai_api_key = ENV.fetch('XAI_API_KEY', 'test')
    end
  end
end

153	        "https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1/projects/#{project_id}/locations/global/publishers/google/models/gemini-2.5-flash:streamGenerateContent?alt=sse"
154	      },

157	          code: 529,

166	      url: lambda {

168	        base = provider.api_base.to_s.sub(/\?.*\z/, '').sub(%r{/+\z}, '')
169	        path = RubyLLM::Providers::Azure::ChatCompletions.new(provider).completion_url
170	        next path if path.start_with?('http')
171	        next base if path.empty?
172	
173	        "#{base}/#{path}"
174	      },
175	      error_response: {
176	        error: {
177	          message: 'The server is temporarily overloaded. Please try again later.',
178	          type: 'server_error',
179	          param: nil,
180	          code: nil
181	        }
182	      },
183	      chunk_status: 500,
184	      expected_error: RubyLLM::ServerError
185	    }
186	  }.freeze
187	
188	  def error_handling_supported?(provider)
189	    ERROR_HANDLING_CONFIGS.key?(provider)
190	  end
191	
192	  def expected_error_for(provider)
193	    ERROR_HANDLING_CONFIGS[provider][:expected_error]
194	  end
195	
196	  def stub_error_response(provider, type)
197	    config = ERROR_HANDLING_CONFIGS[provider]
198	    return unless config
199	
200	    url = config[:url].respond_to?(:call) ? config[:url].call : config[:url]
201	
202	    body = case type
203	           when :chunk
204	             "#{config[:error_response].to_json}\n\n"
205	           when :event
206	             "event: error\ndata: #{config[:error_response].to_json}\n\n"
207	           end
208	
209	    status = type == :chunk ? config[:chunk_status] : 200
210	
211	    stub_request(:post, url)
212	      .to_return(
213	        status: status,
214	        body: body,
215	        headers: { 'Content-Type' => 'text/event-stream' }
216	      )
217	  end
218	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	require 'event_stream_parser'
4	require 'faraday'
5	require 'json'
6	
7	module RubyLLM
8	  # Handles streaming responses from AI providers.
9	  module Streaming
10	    module_function
11	
12	    def stream_response(payload, additional_headers = {}, &block)
13	      accumulator = StreamAccumulator.new
14	      on_data = handle_stream do |chunk|
15	        accumulator.add chunk
16	        block.call chunk
17	      end
18	
19	      response = @connection.post stream_url, payload do |req|
20	        req.headers = additional_headers.merge(req.headers) unless additional_headers.empty?
21	        if faraday_1?
22	          req.options[:on_data] = on_data
23	        else
24	          req.options.on_data = on_data
25	        end
26	      end
27	
28	      message = accumulator.to_message(response)
29	      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Stream completed: #{message.content}" }
30	      message
31	    end
32	
33	    def handle_stream(&block)
34	      build_on_data_handler do |data|
35	        block.call(build_chunk(data)) if data.is_a?(Hash)
36	      end
37	    end
38	
39	    private
40	
41	    def faraday_1?
42	      Faraday::VERSION.start_with?('1')
43	    end
44	
45	    def build_on_data_handler(&handler)
46	      buffer = +''
47	      parser = EventStreamParser::Parser.new
48	
49	      FaradayHandlers.build(
50	        faraday_v1: faraday_1?,
51	        on_chunk: ->(chunk, env) { process_stream_chunk(chunk, parser, env, &handler) },
52	        on_failed_response: ->(chunk, env) { handle_failed_response(chunk, buffer, env) }
53	      )
54	    end
55	
56	    def process_stream_chunk(chunk, parser, env, &)
57	      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Received chunk: #{chunk}" } if RubyLLM.config.log_stream_debug
58	
59	      if error_chunk?(chunk)
60	        handle_error_chunk(chunk, env)
61	      elsif json_error_payload?(chunk)
62	        handle_json_error_chunk(chunk, env)
63	      else
64	        handle_sse(chunk, parser, env, &)
65	      end
66	    end
67	
68	    def error_chunk?(chunk)
69	      chunk.start_with?('event: error')
70	    end
71	
72	    def json_error_payload?(chunk)
73	      chunk.lstrip.start_with?('{') && chunk.include?('"error"')
74	    end
75	
76	    def handle_json_error_chunk(chunk, env)
77	      parse_error_from_json(chunk, env, 'Failed to parse JSON error chunk')
78	    end
79	
80	    def handle_error_chunk(chunk, env)
81	      error_data = chunk.split("\n")[1].delete_prefix('data: ')
82	      parse_error_from_json(error_data, env, 'Failed to parse error chunk')
83	    end
84	
85	    def handle_failed_response(chunk, buffer, env)
86	      buffer << chunk
87	      error_data = JSON.parse(buffer)
88	      raise_stream_error(buffer, error_data, env)
89	    rescue JSON::ParserError
90	      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Accumulating error chunk: #{chunk}" }
91	    end
92	
93	    def handle_sse(chunk, parser, env)
94	      parser.feed(chunk) do |type, data|
95	        case type.to_sym
96	        when :error
97	          handle_error_event(data, env)
98	        else
99	          yield handle_data(data, env) unless data == '[DONE]'
100	        end
101	      end
102	    end
103	
104	    def handle_data(data, env)
105	      parsed = JSON.parse(data)
106	      return parsed unless parsed.is_a?(Hash) && parsed.key?('error')
107	
108	      raise_stream_error(data, parsed, env)
109	    rescue JSON::ParserError => e
110	      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Failed to parse data chunk: #{e.message}" }
111	    end
112	
113	    def handle_error_event(data, env)
114	      parse_error_from_json(data, env, 'Failed to parse error event')
115	    end
116	
117	    def parse_streaming_error(data)
118	      error_data = JSON.parse(data)
119	      [500, error_data['message'] || 'Unknown streaming error']
120	    rescue JSON::ParserError => e
121	      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Failed to parse streaming error: #{e.message}" }
122	      [500, "Failed to parse error: #{data}"]
123	    end
124	
125	    def raise_stream_error(raw_data, parsed_data, env)
126	      status, _message = parse_streaming_error(raw_data)
127	      error_response = build_stream_error_response(parsed_data, env, status)
128	      ErrorMiddleware.parse_error(provider: self, response: error_response)
129	    end
130	
131	    def parse_error_from_json(data, env, error_message)
132	      parsed_data = JSON.parse(data)
133	      raise_stream_error(data, parsed_data, env)
134	    rescue JSON::ParserError => e
135	      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "#{error_message}: #{e.message}" }
136	    end
137	
138	    def build_stream_error_response(parsed_data, env, status)
139	      error_status = status || env&.status || 500
140	
141	      if faraday_1?
142	        Struct.new(:body, :status).new(parsed_data, error_status)
143	      else
144	        env.merge(body: parsed_data, status: error_status)
145	      end
146	    end
147	
148	    # Builds Faraday on_data handlers for different major versions.
149	    module FaradayHandlers
150	      module_function
151	
152	      def build(faraday_v1:, on_chunk:, on_failed_response:)
153	        if faraday_v1
154	          v1_on_data(on_chunk)
155	        else
156	          v2_on_data(on_chunk, on_failed_response)
157	        end
158	      end
159	
160	      def v1_on_data(on_chunk)
161	        proc do |chunk, _size|
162	          on_chunk.call(chunk, nil)
163	        end
164	      end
165	
166	      def v2_on_data(on_chunk, on_failed_response)
167	        proc do |chunk, _bytes, env|
168	          if env&.status == 200
169	            on_chunk.call(chunk, env)
170	          else
171	            on_failed_response.call(chunk, env)
172	          end
173	        end
174	      end
175	    end
176	  end
177	end
178

require 'event_stream_parser'
require 'faraday'
require 'json'

module RubyLLM
  # Handles streaming responses from AI providers.
  module Streaming
    module_function

    def stream_response(payload, additional_headers = {}, &block)
      accumulator = StreamAccumulator.new
      on_data = handle_stream do |chunk|
        accumulator.add chunk
        block.call chunk
      end

      response = @connection.post stream_url, payload do |req|
        req.headers = additional_headers.merge(req.headers) unless additional_headers.empty?
        if faraday_1?
          req.options[:on_data] = on_data
        else
          req.options.on_data = on_data
        end
      end

      message = accumulator.to_message(response)
      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Stream completed: #{message.content}" }
      message
    end

    def handle_stream(&block)
      build_on_data_handler do |data|
        block.call(build_chunk(data)) if data.is_a?(Hash)
      end
    end

    private

    def faraday_1?
      Faraday::VERSION.start_with?('1')
    end

    def build_on_data_handler(&handler)
      buffer = +''
      parser = EventStreamParser::Parser.new

      FaradayHandlers.build(
        faraday_v1: faraday_1?,
        on_chunk: ->(chunk, env) { process_stream_chunk(chunk, parser, env, &handler) },
        on_failed_response: ->(chunk, env) { handle_failed_response(chunk, buffer, env) }
      )
    end

    def process_stream_chunk(chunk, parser, env, &)
      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Received chunk: #{chunk}" } if RubyLLM.config.log_stream_debug

      if error_chunk?(chunk)
        handle_error_chunk(chunk, env)
      elsif json_error_payload?(chunk)
        handle_json_error_chunk(chunk, env)
      else
        handle_sse(chunk, parser, env, &)
      end
    end

    def error_chunk?(chunk)
      chunk.start_with?('event: error')
    end

    def json_error_payload?(chunk)
      chunk.lstrip.start_with?('{') && chunk.include?('"error"')
    end

    def handle_json_error_chunk(chunk, env)
      parse_error_from_json(chunk, env, 'Failed to parse JSON error chunk')
    end

    def handle_error_chunk(chunk, env)
      error_data = chunk.split("\n")[1].delete_prefix('data: ')
      parse_error_from_json(error_data, env, 'Failed to parse error chunk')
    end

    def handle_failed_response(chunk, buffer, env)
      buffer << chunk
      error_data = JSON.parse(buffer)
      raise_stream_error(buffer, error_data, env)
    rescue JSON::ParserError
      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Accumulating error chunk: #{chunk}" }
    end

    def handle_sse(chunk, parser, env)
      parser.feed(chunk) do |type, data|
        case type.to_sym
        when :error
          handle_error_event(data, env)
        else
          yield handle_data(data, env) unless data == '[DONE]'
        end
      end
    end

    def handle_data(data, env)
      parsed = JSON.parse(data)
      return parsed unless parsed.is_a?(Hash) && parsed.key?('error')

      raise_stream_error(data, parsed, env)
    rescue JSON::ParserError => e
      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Failed to parse data chunk: #{e.message}" }
    end

    def handle_error_event(data, env)
      parse_error_from_json(data, env, 'Failed to parse error event')
    end

    def parse_streaming_error(data)
      error_data = JSON.parse(data)
      [500, error_data['message'] || 'Unknown streaming error']
    rescue JSON::ParserError => e
      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Failed to parse streaming error: #{e.message}" }
      [500, "Failed to parse error: #{data}"]
    end

    def raise_stream_error(raw_data, parsed_data, env)
      status, _message = parse_streaming_error(raw_data)
      error_response = build_stream_error_response(parsed_data, env, status)
      ErrorMiddleware.parse_error(provider: self, response: error_response)
    end

    def parse_error_from_json(data, env, error_message)
      parsed_data = JSON.parse(data)
      raise_stream_error(data, parsed_data, env)
    rescue JSON::ParserError => e
      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "#{error_message}: #{e.message}" }
    end

    def build_stream_error_response(parsed_data, env, status)
      error_status = status || env&.status || 500

      if faraday_1?
        Struct.new(:body, :status).new(parsed_data, error_status)
      else
        env.merge(body: parsed_data, status: error_status)
      end
    end

    # Builds Faraday on_data handlers for different major versions.
    module FaradayHandlers
      module_function

      def build(faraday_v1:, on_chunk:, on_failed_response:)
        if faraday_v1
          v1_on_data(on_chunk)
        else
          v2_on_data(on_chunk, on_failed_response)
        end
      end

      def v1_on_data(on_chunk)
        proc do |chunk, _size|
          on_chunk.call(chunk, nil)
        end
      end

      def v2_on_data(on_chunk, on_failed_response)
        proc do |chunk, _bytes, env|
          if env&.status == 200
            on_chunk.call(chunk, env)
          else
            on_failed_response.call(chunk, env)
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Here are the answers for each step, with `file:line` references throughout.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What it is:** `RubyLLM` is a Ruby gem providing one unified interface (`RubyLLM.chat`, `.embed`, `.paint`, `.moderate`, `.transcribe`) over many external LLM services. Zeitwerk-autoloaded gem (`lib/ruby_llm.rb:17-39`).

**Code organization (`lib/ruby_llm/`):**
- **Public surface / orchestration:** `chat.rb`, `embedding.rb`, `image.rb`, `moderation.rb`, `transcription.rb`, top-level dispatch in `ruby_llm.rb:43-101`.
- **Two-layer integration architecture — the key separation:**
  - **`provider.rb`** — `RubyLLM::Provider` base class. A provider knows *where* to talk (host, auth/headers, config) and *who* it is. Holds the provider registry + protocol registry (class methods, `provider.rb:126-206`).
  - **`protocol.rb`** — `RubyLLM::Protocol` base class. A protocol knows *how* to talk: render payloads, parse responses, endpoints, streaming.
- **`providers/`** — one `<name>.rb` per integration (13 of them) + a `<name>/` subdir of customization mixins.
- **`protocols/`** — the shared API *dialects*: `chat_completions.rb` (the OpenAI-compatible lingua franca, used by most), plus `anthropic.rb`, `gemini.rb`, `responses.rb`, `converse.rb` (Bedrock), each with a subdir of submodules.
- **Plumbing:** `connection.rb` (Faraday stack), `error.rb` + `error_middleware.rb` (typed errors), `configuration.rb` (settings), `models.rb` + `models.json` + `aliases.json` + `model_registry.rb` (model catalog), `streaming.rb`/`stream_accumulator.rb`.
- **`active_record/`, `railtie.rb`, `generators/`** — Rails integration (autoload-ignored at `ruby_llm.rb:35-38`).

**Where integrations live:** `lib/ruby_llm/providers/*.rb` (+ subdirs), built on `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb` and the shared dialects in `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/`. All are registered in `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116`.

**Specs:** `spec/ruby_llm/` mirrors lib; cross-provider test matrix in `spec/support/models_to_test.rb`.

---

## Step 2 — Request routing to the concrete integration

Order of layers for a chat request:

1. `RubyLLM.chat(...)` → `Chat.new` (`ruby_llm.rb:58`, `chat.rb:12`).
2. `Chat#with_model` resolves model + provider: `Models.resolve(model_id, ...)` (`chat.rb:84-88`).
3. `Models.resolve` (`models.rb:132-156`) — looks up the `Model::Info` in the registry (`models.rb:152`), reads its `provider` slug, then does the provider lookup **`Provider.providers[model.provider.to_sym]`** (`models.rb:153`) and **instantiates** it: `provider_class.new(config)` (`models.rb:155`). `Provider#initialize` calls `ensure_configured!` then builds the `Connection` (`provider.rb:13-17`).
4. `Chat#ask` → `complete` → `provider_completion` → **`@provider.complete(...)`** (`chat.rb:296-310`).
5. `Provider#complete` (`provider.rb:54-69`) selects the protocol via `resolve_protocol` (`provider.rb:210-213`): explicit `with_protocol` / `config.<slug>_protocol` wins, else `protocol_for(model, **)` (`provider.rb:49-51`, overridable — e.g. `OpenAI#protocol_for` at `openai.rb:15-17`). The protocol class is looked up in the per-provider table `protocols` (`provider.rb:163-170`, `fetch_protocol` at `provider.rb:223-227`).
6. `protocol_class.new(self, model).complete(...)` (`provider.rb:57`) → `Protocol#complete` (`protocol.rb:20-45`) renders payload and calls `sync_response`/`stream_response`, which `POST`s via `Connection#post` (`protocol.rb:109-114`, `connection.rb:40-47`) to `completion_url` using the provider's `headers`/`api_base`.

The two registries: provider lookup table = `Provider.providers` (`provider.rb:183-185`); protocol lookup table per provider = `Provider#protocols` (`provider.rb:168-170`).

---

## Step 3 — The shared Chat Completions dialect and its customizations

**Shared base:** `RubyLLM::Protocols::ChatCompletions` (`protocols/chat_completions.rb`) includes these submodules (all under `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/`):
- `Chat` (`chat.rb`) — `completion_url` `'chat/completions'`, `render_payload`, `parse_completion_response`, `format_messages`/`format_role`/`format_content`, token accounting, citations, thinking extraction.
- `Embeddings` (`embeddings.rb`), `Models` (`models.rb` — `models_url` `'models'`, `parse_list_models_response`), `Moderation`, `Streaming` (`streaming.rb` — `build_chunk`, `parse_streaming_error`), `Tools`, `Images`, `Media`, `Transcription`.
- `maybe_normalize_temperature` → `Temperature.normalize` (`chat_completions.rb:17-19`, `temperature.rb`).

A new provider gets all of this for free by subclassing `Protocols::ChatCompletions` and `include`-ing override modules. **Every existing customization (a feature the new one must consciously decide on):**

**DeepSeek** (`providers/deepseek.rb` + `deepseek/chat.rb`): overrides `format_role` (plain `to_s`, no system→developer mapping) and `format_content` (text only — no docs/images/audio).

**xAI** (`providers/xai.rb` + `xai/chat.rb`, `xai/models.rb`): `format_role` plain; `format_content` images-only; custom `parse_list_models_response` (grok families, image/video model detection); adds `Content-Type` header; **no `capabilities`** class method.

**Mistral** (`providers/mistral.rb` + `mistral/{chat,media,models,embeddings}.rb`): `format_role` plain; custom `format_messages` + `render_payload` (calls `super`, deletes `stream_options`, configures thinking via `prompt_mode`/`reasoning_effort`, normalizes `:required`→`'any'`/single-fn tool_choice via `build_tool_choice`/`normalize_required_tool_choice`); thinking content-blocks; custom `Media` (image_url/document_url), `Models`, `Embeddings`.

**Perplexity** (`providers/perplexity.rb` + `perplexity/{chat,media,models}.rb`): `format_role` plain; `format_content` via own Media (image/pdf/doc/text, extension allow-list); **`Models` hardcodes a static `MODEL_IDS` list and overrides `list_models`** (`perplexity/models.rb:18-21`); adds `Content-Type`; **overrides `Provider#parse_error`** to scrape HTML `<title>` for auth errors (`perplexity.rb:26-40`).

**Ollama** (`providers/ollama.rb` + `ollama/{chat,media,models}.rb`): `local? = true`; `api_base` required, key optional; `format_role` plain; image-only Media with `detail: 'auto'`; custom `Models`.

**GPUStack** (`providers/gpustack.rb` + `gpustack/models.rb`): **inherits another provider's dialect** — `class ChatCompletions < Ollama::ChatCompletions` then `include GPUStack::Models` (`gpustack.rb:8-10`). `local? = true`; `api_base` required; uses `Ollama::Capabilities`; custom `Models` (parses `items`, `meta`).

**OpenRouter** (`providers/openrouter.rb` + `openrouter/{chat,images,models,streaming}.rb`): `render_payload` calls `super`, deletes `reasoning_effort`, strips schema `strict`, adds `reasoning` object; overrides `format_thinking`/`extract_thinking_text`/`extract_thinking_signature` (reasoning_details); custom `build_chunk` (`openrouter/streaming.rb`); custom `Images`, `Models`; **overrides `Provider#parse_error`** (nested metadata.raw, `openrouter.rb:27-41`).

**Azure** (`providers/azure.rb` + `azure/{chat_completions,chat,embeddings,media,models}.rb`): `protocol :chat_completions, Azure::ChatCompletions` (top-level class, not nested); dynamic endpoint computation from api_base shape (`azure/chat_completions.rb`); `completion_url`→`azure_endpoint(:chat)` (`azure/chat.rb`); custom `headers` (`api-key` vs Bearer); overrides class `configured?` and `assume_models_exist? = true`; **overrides instance `ensure_configured!`** (`azure.rb:38-50`).

**OpenAI** itself (`providers/openai.rb`): registers **two** protocols (`:responses` default + `:chat_completions`) and overrides `protocol_for` to route audio/realtime/search-preview models to Chat Completions (`openai.rb:7-17`).

**Temperature normalization** (`chat_completions/temperature.rb`) is OpenAI-specific (o-series/gpt-5 force `1.0`, `-search` drops temperature) — a non-OpenAI provider may need to override `maybe_normalize_temperature` (as defined on the base `Protocol#maybe_normalize_temperature`, `protocol.rb:78-80`).

---

## Step 4 — Declaring & validating settings

- **System options** declared in `Configuration` via `option` (`configuration.rb:10-23`, declarations `configuration.rb:43-70`).
- **Per-integration settings declared** in each provider class as `self.configuration_options` and `self.configuration_requirements` (base defaults `provider.rb:139-145`; e.g. DeepSeek `deepseek.rb:29-35`, OpenAI `openai.rb:32-44`, Azure `azure.rb:18-36`).
- **Registered** at boot: `Provider.register` (`provider.rb:174-177`) calls `Configuration.register_provider_options(configuration_options + [:"#{name}_protocol"])` → `Configuration.register_provider_options` (`configuration.rb:25-27`) defines a config accessor per option. Triggered by the `Provider.register` calls in `ruby_llm.rb:104-116`.
- **Validation that they're configured:**
  - Class predicate `Provider.configured?(config)` = all requirements present (`provider.rb:159-161`).
  - At instantiation: `Provider#initialize` → `ensure_configured!` raises `ConfigurationError` with a generated config block if a requirement is missing (`provider.rb:13-15`, `237-249`). Azure overrides this (`azure.rb:38-50`).
  - Registry/refresh filters via `configured_providers` / `configured_remote_providers` (`provider.rb:195-205`, used in `models.rb:119`).

---

## Step 5 — HTTP failures → typed errors

- **Where raw response becomes typed error:** `RubyLLM::ErrorMiddleware`, a Faraday response middleware registered as `:llm_errors` (`error_middleware.rb:90`) and installed in the connection stack at `connection.rb:117`. On each completed response, `ErrorMiddleware.parse_error(provider:, response:)` runs (`error_middleware.rb:14-18, 41-71`).
- **The typed-error family:** all subclass `RubyLLM::Error` (`error.rb:6-22`), each with a `default_message`: `BadRequestError`, `ForbiddenError`, `ContextLengthExceededError`, `OverloadedError`, `PaymentRequiredError`, `RateLimitError`, `ServerError`, `ServiceUnavailableError`, `UnauthorizedError` (`error.rb:44-104`). Plus non-HTTP `ConfigurationError`, `ModelNotFoundError`, etc. (`error.rb:25-29`) and `UnsupportedAttachmentError` (`error.rb:32-40`).
- **Status → error mapping:** `case response.status` in `error_middleware.rb:44-70`: 400→Bad/ContextLength (pattern match `error_middleware.rb:21-32, 75-79`), 401→Unauthorized, 402→Payment, 403→Forbidden, 429→RateLimit/ContextLength (`error_middleware.rb:57-61`, patterns `34-39, 81-85`), 500→Server, 502-504→ServiceUnavailable, 529→Overloaded, else→`Error`.
- **Per-integration customization of error *reading*:** the message text comes from `provider&.parse_error(response)` (`error_middleware.rb:42`). Default `Provider#parse_error` parses JSON shapes (`provider.rb:104-124`). Overridden by **Perplexity** (HTML `<title>`, `perplexity.rb:26-40`) and **OpenRouter** (nested `metadata.raw`, `openrouter.rb:27-55`). Inline parse error also raised in `chat_completions/chat.rb:66`.
- **Streaming errors** take a parallel path: `Streaming#raise_stream_error` builds a synthetic response and calls the same `ErrorMiddleware.parse_error` (`streaming.rb:125-129`); status derived via `parse_streaming_error` (base `streaming.rb:117-123`, overridable — see `chat_completions/streaming.rb:47-59`).
- **Retry coupling:** `RateLimitError`/`ServerError`/`ServiceUnavailableError`/`OverloadedError` are in `Connection#retry_exceptions` (`connection.rb:126-138`).

---

## Step 6 — Every site that must change to add an integration

1. **Provider class file** — create `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<name>.rb` (+ `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<name>/` mixins).
2. **Registration** — add `RubyLLM::Provider.register :<name>, RubyLLM::Providers::<Class>` in `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116`. This auto-wires config options (Step 4).
3. **Zeitwerk inflector** — if the class name isn't a plain capitalize of the slug (e.g. `OpenAI`, `DeepSeek`, `GPUStack`, `XAI`, `VertexAI`), add an entry to `loader.inflector.inflect(...)` in `lib/ruby_llm.rb:18-34`.
4. **Model catalog** — the lookup table consumed at `models.rb:152-153`:
   - `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` — add the provider's models (or they'll only appear after `refresh!`); and/or `lib/ruby_llm/aliases.json` (`aliases.rb`).
   - If the provider is on models.dev: add to `MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP` (`models.rb:11-22`).
   - Add the slug to `PROVIDER_PREFERENCE` (`models.rb:26-40`) so ambiguous model ids resolve sanely.
5. **Test matrix** — add entries in `spec/support/models_to_test.rb` (`CHAT_MODELS:10-25`, and as applicable `STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_MODELS`, `THINKING_MODELS`, `VISION_MODELS`, `EMBEDDING_MODELS`, etc.); if local, add to `LOCAL_PROVIDER_SLUGS` (`models_to_test.rb:4`).
6. **Test config** — add api key/base lines in `spec/support/rubyllm_configuration.rb:14-42`.
7. **Streaming error fixture** — add a `<name>:` entry to `ERROR_HANDLING_CONFIGS` in `spec/support/streaming_error_helpers.rb:4-186`.
8. **Docs** — `docs/_getting_started/configuration.md` (config lines, ~`:37-107`) and `docs/_reference/available-models.md`; `.env.example`.

---

## Step 7 — Complete edit map

Assume new integration slug `acme`, class `RubyLLM::Providers::Acme`, speaking the Chat Completions dialect.

### Decisions to make per the Step-3 customization checklist (override in `acme/*` only where Acme deviates)
- `parse_error` (override on the provider class, `provider.rb:104` — like Perplexity/OpenRouter) if errors aren't standard JSON.
- `maybe_normalize_temperature` / a `Temperature`-style module if temperature rules differ (base `protocol.rb:78`).

- `protocol_for` only if routing to multiple protocols (OpenAI).

No changes are needed to `configuration.rb` (options auto-register via `Provider.register`) or `error.rb`/`error_middleware.rb` (status mapping is shared; only message *reading* is per-provider).

result:
I now have a complete picture. Here are the answers for each step, with `file:line` references throughout.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What it is:** `RubyLLM` is a Ruby gem providing one unified interface (`RubyLLM.chat`, `.embed`, `.paint`, `.moderate`, `.transcribe`) over many external LLM services. Zeitwerk-autoloaded gem (`lib/ruby_llm.rb:17-39`).

**Code organization (`lib/ruby_llm/`):**
- **Public surface / orchestration:** `chat.rb`, `embedding.rb`, `image.rb`, `moderation.rb`, `transcription.rb`, top-level dispatch in `ruby_llm.rb:43-101`.
- **Two-layer integration architecture — the key separation:**
  - **`provider.rb`** — `RubyLLM::Provider` base class. A provider knows *where* to talk (host, auth/headers, config) and *who* it is. Holds the provider registry + protocol registry (class methods, `provider.rb:126-206`).
  - **`protocol.rb`** — `RubyLLM::Protocol` base class. A protocol knows *how* to talk: render payloads, parse responses, endpoints, streaming.
- **`providers/`** — one `<name>.rb` per integration (13 of them) + a `<name>/` subdir of customization mixins.
- **`protocols/`** — the shared API *dialects*: `chat_completions.rb` (the OpenAI-compatible lingua franca, used by most), plus `anthropic.rb`, `gemini.rb`, `responses.rb`, `converse.rb` (Bedrock), each with a subdir of submodules.
- **Plumbing:** `connection.rb` (Faraday stack), `error.rb` + `error_middleware.rb` (typed errors), `configuration.rb` (settings), `models.rb` + `models.json` + `aliases.json` + `model_registry.rb` (model catalog), `streaming.rb`/`stream_accumulator.rb`.
- **`active_record/`, `railtie.rb`, `generators/`** — Rails integration (autoload-ignored at `ruby_llm.rb:35-38`).

**Where integrations live:** `lib/ruby_llm/providers/*.rb` (+ subdirs), built on `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb` and the shared dialects in `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/`. All are registered in `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116`.

**Specs:** `spec/ruby_llm/` mirrors lib; cross-provider test matrix in `spec/support/models_to_test.rb`.

---

## Step 2 — Request routing to the concrete integration

Order of layers for a chat request:

1. `RubyLLM.chat(...)` → `Chat.new` (`ruby_llm.rb:58`, `chat.rb:12`).
2. `Chat#with_model` resolves model + provider: `Models.resolve(model_id, ...)` (`chat.rb:84-88`).
3. `Models.resolve` (`models.rb:132-156`) — looks up the `Model::Info` in the registry (`models.rb:152`), reads its `provider` slug, then does the provider lookup **`Provider.providers[model.provider.to_sym]`** (`models.rb:153`) and **instantiates** it: `provider_class.new(config)` (`models.rb:155`). `Provider#initialize` calls `ensure_configured!` then builds the `Connection` (`provider.rb:13-17`).
4. `Chat#ask` → `complete` → `provider_completion` → **`@provider.complete(...)`** (`chat.rb:296-310`).
5. `Provider#complete` (`provider.rb:54-69`) selects the protocol via `resolve_protocol` (`provider.rb:210-213`): explicit `with_protocol` / `config.<slug>_protocol` wins, else `protocol_for(model, **)` (`provider.rb:49-51`, overridable — e.g. `OpenAI#protocol_for` at `openai.rb:15-17`). The protocol class is looked up in the per-provider table `protocols` (`provider.rb:163-170`, `fetch_protocol` at `provider.rb:223-227`).
6. `protocol_class.new(self, model).complete(...)` (`provider.rb:57`) → `Protocol#complete` (`protocol.rb:20-45`) renders payload and calls `sync_response`/`stream_response`, which `POST`s via `Connection#post` (`protocol.rb:109-114`, `connection.rb:40-47`) to `completion_url` using the provider's `headers`/`api_base`.

The two registries: provider lookup table = `Provider.providers` (`provider.rb:183-185`); protocol lookup table per provider = `Provider#protocols` (`provider.rb:168-170`).

---

## Step 3 — The shared Chat Completions dialect and its customizations

**Shared base:** `RubyLLM::Protocols::ChatCompletions` (`protocols/chat_completions.rb`) includes these submodules (all under `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/`):
- `Chat` (`chat.rb`) — `completion_url` `'chat/completions'`, `render_payload`, `parse_completion_response`, `format_messages`/`format_role`/`format_content`, token accounting, citations, thinking extraction.
- `Embeddings` (`embeddings.rb`), `Models` (`models.rb` — `models_url` `'models'`, `parse_list_models_response`), `Moderation`, `Streaming` (`streaming.rb` — `build_chunk`, `parse_streaming_error`), `Tools`, `Images`, `Media`, `Transcription`.
- `maybe_normalize_temperature` → `Temperature.normalize` (`chat_completions.rb:17-19`, `temperature.rb`).

A new provider gets all of this for free by subclassing `Protocols::ChatCompletions` and `include`-ing override modules. **Every existing customization (a feature the new one must consciously decide on):**

**DeepSeek** (`providers/deepseek.rb` + `deepseek/chat.rb`): overrides `format_role` (plain `to_s`, no system→developer mapping) and `format_content` (text only — no docs/images/audio).

**xAI** (`providers/xai.rb` + `xai/chat.rb`, `xai/models.rb`): `format_role` plain; `format_content` images-only; custom `parse_list_models_response` (grok families, image/video model detection); adds `Content-Type` header; **no `capabilities`** class method.

**Mistral** (`providers/mistral.rb` + `mistral/{chat,media,models,embeddings}.rb`): `format_role` plain; custom `format_messages` + `render_payload` (calls `super`, deletes `stream_options`, configures thinking via `prompt_mode`/`reasoning_effort`, normalizes `:required`→`'any'`/single-fn tool_choice via `build_tool_choice`/`normalize_required_tool_choice`); thinking content-blocks; custom `Media` (image_url/document_url), `Models`, `Embeddings`.

**Perplexity** (`providers/perplexity.rb` + `perplexity/{chat,media,models}.rb`): `format_role` plain; `format_content` via own Media (image/pdf/doc/text, extension allow-list); **`Models` hardcodes a static `MODEL_IDS` list and overrides `list_models`** (`perplexity/models.rb:18-21`); adds `Content-Type`; **overrides `Provider#parse_error`** to scrape HTML `<title>` for auth errors (`perplexity.rb:26-40`).

**Ollama** (`providers/ollama.rb` + `ollama/{chat,media,models}.rb`): `local? = true`; `api_base` required, key optional; `format_role` plain; image-only Media with `detail: 'auto'`; custom `Models`.

**GPUStack** (`providers/gpustack.rb` + `gpustack/models.rb`): **inherits another provider's dialect** — `class ChatCompletions < Ollama::ChatCompletions` then `include GPUStack::Models` (`gpustack.rb:8-10`). `local? = true`; `api_base` required; uses `Ollama::Capabilities`; custom `Models` (parses `items`, `meta`).

**OpenRouter** (`providers/openrouter.rb` + `openrouter/{chat,images,models,streaming}.rb`): `render_payload` calls `super`, deletes `reasoning_effort`, strips schema `strict`, adds `reasoning` object; overrides `format_thinking`/`extract_thinking_text`/`extract_thinking_signature` (reasoning_details); custom `build_chunk` (`openrouter/streaming.rb`); custom `Images`, `Models`; **overrides `Provider#parse_error`** (nested metadata.raw, `openrouter.rb:27-41`).

**Azure** (`providers/azure.rb` + `azure/{chat_completions,chat,embeddings,media,models}.rb`): `protocol :chat_completions, Azure::ChatCompletions` (top-level class, not nested); dynamic endpoint computation from api_base shape (`azure/chat_completions.rb`); `completion_url`→`azure_endpoint(:chat)` (`azure/chat.rb`); custom `headers` (`api-key` vs Bearer); overrides class `configured?` and `assume_models_exist? = true`; **overrides instance `ensure_configured!`** (`azure.rb:38-50`).

**OpenAI** itself (`providers/openai.rb`): registers **two** protocols (`:responses` default + `:chat_completions`) and overrides `protocol_for` to route audio/realtime/search-preview models to Chat Completions (`openai.rb:7-17`).

**Temperature normalization** (`chat_completions/temperature.rb`) is OpenAI-specific (o-series/gpt-5 force `1.0`, `-search` drops temperature) — a non-OpenAI provider may need to override `maybe_normalize_temperature` (as defined on the base `Protocol#maybe_normalize_temperature`, `protocol.rb:78-80`).

---

## Step 4 — Declaring & validating settings

- **System options** declared in `Configuration` via `option` (`configuration.rb:10-23`, declarations `configuration.rb:43-70`).
- **Per-integration settings declared** in each provider class as `self.configuration_options` and `self.configuration_requirements` (base defaults `provider.rb:139-145`; e.g. DeepSeek `deepseek.rb:29-35`, OpenAI `openai.rb:32-44`, Azure `azure.rb:18-36`).
- **Registered** at boot: `Provider.register` (`provider.rb:174-177`) calls `Configuration.register_provider_options(configuration_options + [:"#{name}_protocol"])` → `Configuration.register_provider_options` (`configuration.rb:25-27`) defines a config accessor per option. Triggered by the `Provider.register` calls in `ruby_llm.rb:104-116`.
- **Validation that they're configured:**
  - Class predicate `Provider.configured?(config)` = all requirements present (`provider.rb:159-161`).
  - At instantiation: `Provider#initialize` → `ensure_configured!` raises `ConfigurationError` with a generated config block if a requirement is missing (`provider.rb:13-15`, `237-249`). Azure overrides this (`azure.rb:38-50`).
  - Registry/refresh filters via `configured_providers` / `configured_remote_providers` (`provider.rb:195-205`, used in `models.rb:119`).

---

## Step 5 — HTTP failures → typed errors

- **Where raw response becomes typed error:** `RubyLLM::ErrorMiddleware`, a Faraday response middleware registered as `:llm_errors` (`error_middleware.rb:90`) and installed in the connection stack at `connection.rb:117`. On each completed response, `ErrorMiddleware.parse_error(provider:, response:)` runs (`error_middleware.rb:14-18, 41-71`).
- **The typed-error family:** all subclass `RubyLLM::Error` (`error.rb:6-22`), each with a `default_message`: `BadRequestError`, `ForbiddenError`, `ContextLengthExceededError`, `OverloadedError`, `PaymentRequiredError`, `RateLimitError`, `ServerError`, `ServiceUnavailableError`, `UnauthorizedError` (`error.rb:44-104`). Plus non-HTTP `ConfigurationError`, `ModelNotFoundError`, etc. (`error.rb:25-29`) and `UnsupportedAttachmentError` (`error.rb:32-40`).
- **Status → error mapping:** `case response.status` in `error_middleware.rb:44-70`: 400→Bad/ContextLength (pattern match `error_middleware.rb:21-32, 75-79`), 401→Unauthorized, 402→Payment, 403→Forbidden, 429→RateLimit/ContextLength (`error_middleware.rb:57-61`, patterns `34-39, 81-85`), 500→Server, 502-504→ServiceUnavailable, 529→Overloaded, else→`Error`.
- **Per-integration customization of error *reading*:** the message text comes from `provider&.parse_error(response)` (`error_middleware.rb:42`). Default `Provider#parse_error` parses JSON shapes (`provider.rb:104-124`). Overridden by **Perplexity** (HTML `<title>`, `perplexity.rb:26-40`) and **OpenRouter** (nested `metadata.raw`, `openrouter.rb:27-55`). Inline parse error also raised in `chat_completions/chat.rb:66`.
- **Streaming errors** take a parallel path: `Streaming#raise_stream_error` builds a synthetic response and calls the same `ErrorMiddleware.parse_error` (`streaming.rb:125-129`); status derived via `parse_streaming_error` (base `streaming.rb:117-123`, overridable — see `chat_completions/streaming.rb:47-59`).
- **Retry coupling:** `RateLimitError`/`ServerError`/`ServiceUnavailableError`/`OverloadedError` are in `Connection#retry_exceptions` (`connection.rb:126-138`).

---

## Step 6 — Every site that must change to add an integration

1. **Provider class file** — create `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<name>.rb` (+ `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<name>/` mixins).
2. **Registration** — add `RubyLLM::Provider.register :<name>, RubyLLM::Providers::<Class>` in `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116`. This auto-wires config options (Step 4).
3. **Zeitwerk inflector** — if the class name isn't a plain capitalize of the slug (e.g. `OpenAI`, `DeepSeek`, `GPUStack`, `XAI`, `VertexAI`), add an entry to `loader.inflector.inflect(...)` in `lib/ruby_llm.rb:18-34`.
4. **Model catalog** — the lookup table consumed at `models.rb:152-153`:
   - `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` — add the provider's models (or they'll only appear after `refresh!`); and/or `lib/ruby_llm/aliases.json` (`aliases.rb`).
   - If the provider is on models.dev: add to `MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP` (`models.rb:11-22`).
   - Add the slug to `PROVIDER_PREFERENCE` (`models.rb:26-40`) so ambiguous model ids resolve sanely.
5. **Test matrix** — add entries in `spec/support/models_to_test.rb` (`CHAT_MODELS:10-25`, and as applicable `STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_MODELS`, `THINKING_MODELS`, `VISION_MODELS`, `EMBEDDING_MODELS`, etc.); if local, add to `LOCAL_PROVIDER_SLUGS` (`models_to_test.rb:4`).
6. **Test config** — add api key/base lines in `spec/support/rubyllm_configuration.rb:14-42`.
7. **Streaming error fixture** — add a `<name>:` entry to `ERROR_HANDLING_CONFIGS` in `spec/support/streaming_error_helpers.rb:4-186`.
8. **Docs** — `docs/_getting_started/configuration.md` (config lines, ~`:37-107`) and `docs/_reference/available-models.md`; `.env.example`.

---

### Decisions to make per the Step-3 customization checklist (override in `acme/*` only where Acme deviates)
- `parse_error` (override on the provider class, `provider.rb:104` — like Perplexity/OpenRouter) if errors aren't standard JSON.
- `maybe_normalize_temperature` / a `Temperature`-style module if temperature rules differ (base `protocol.rb:78`).
- `render_payload` super-call tweaks (thinking, schema strict, stream_options) — like Mistral/OpenRouter.
- `build_chunk` / `parse_streaming_error` if streaming shape differs (`chat_completions/streaming.rb`).
- `local?`, `assume_models_exist?`, custom `configured?`/`ensure_configured!` if auth model differs (Azure/Ollama/GPUStack).
- `protocol_for` only if routing to multiple protocols (OpenAI).

No changes are needed to `configuration.rb` (options auto-register via `Provider.register`) or `error.rb`/`error_middleware.rb` (status mapping is shared; only message *reading* is per-provider).
